mission.title: Install and Configure Kmesh on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kmesh is a high-performance service mesh data plane based on eBPF and programmable kernel, designed to enhance traffic management, security, and monitoring without requiring changes to application code. Installing Kmesh allows you to leverage its low overhead and sidecarless architecture for efficient service communication.

steps.title: Install Kmesh from the repository Helm chart
steps.description:
Clone the Kmesh repository and install:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/kmesh-net/kmesh.git
cd kmesh
kubectl create namespace kmesh-system
helm install kmesh deploy/charts/kmesh-helm --namespace kmesh-system
```
See https://kmesh.net/en/docs/setup/quick-start/ for details.

steps.title: Enable Kmesh for a namespace
steps.description:
Label the namespace to enable Kmesh sidecarless mesh:
```bash
kubectl label namespace default istio.io/dataplane-mode=Kmesh
```

steps.title: Deploy a test application
steps.description:
Deploy a sample app to test traffic management:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/istio/istio/release-1.21/samples/bookinfo/platform/kube/bookinfo.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kmesh-system
kubectl get ds -n kmesh-system
```
Kmesh runs as a DaemonSet with eBPF programs on each node.

resolution.summary: Kmesh is installed as a high-performance eBPF-based service mesh without sidecars.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kmesh
uninstall.description:
```bash
kubectl label namespace default istio.io/dataplane-mode-
helm uninstall kmesh -n kmesh-system
kubectl delete namespace kmesh-system
```

upgrade.title: Pull latest and upgrade
upgrade.description:
```bash
cd kmesh && git pull
helm upgrade kmesh deploy/charts/kmesh-helm --namespace kmesh-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Kmesh DaemonSet pods failing
troubleshooting.description:
Kmesh requires Linux kernel 5.10+ with BTF support:
```bash
uname -r
ls /sys/kernel/btf/vmlinux
```

troubleshooting.title: eBPF programs not loading
troubleshooting.description:
Check Kmesh daemon logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=kmesh -n kmesh-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Traffic not being intercepted
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the namespace label:
```bash
kubectl get namespace default --show-labels
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster version 1.24 or higher and kubectl installed on your machine.
